summary: Populate nodes.definition on is_new_concept accept (CLI + future web parity)

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: review_tags.py:74-77 ensures the concept node exists via INSERT OR IGNORE INTO nodes(id, type, label) — definition column is never written. staged_tags.new_concept_def, the LLM-proposed definition the human read as 'NEW DEF: ...' on review, is silently discarded on accept. 14 is_new_concept=1 rows pending in the live DB, 0 accepted yet, so the path has never fired in production. All 44 existing concept nodes have populated definitions (seeded from concepts/taxonomy.toml). This is a prereq for the web-review parity harness (docs/web-review/impl.md P7b) — fixing in CLI keeps strict parity, avoiding a known divergence in the harness fixture for is_new_concept=1 accepts.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Switch from INSERT OR IGNORE to ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE with COALESCE on the definition column:

INSERT INTO nodes(id, type, label, definition)
VALUES(?, 'concept', ?, ?)
ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = COALESCE(nodes.definition, excluded.definition)

COALESCE preserves any existing non-null definition (taxonomy-seeded concepts safe), only fills in NULL/missing. Caller passes row['new_concept_def'] which is None for is_new_concept=0 rows — harmless. Add unit tests in tests/test_promote_definition.py covering: (a) new is_new_concept=1 accept populates definition, (b) existing concept's definition not clobbered, (c) is_new_concept=0 accept with None def is a no-op on definition column. Update docs/web-review/impl.md §9.7 and design.md §9.7 to mark resolved.

resolution.note: promote_to_expresses now upserts with COALESCE(nodes.definition, excluded.definition); LLM-proposed new_concept_def lands on new concept nodes, pre-existing taxonomy definitions preserved. 5 regression tests (test_promote_definition.py) cover new accept, no-clobber, NULL backfill, is_new_concept=0 path, and edge-tier sanity. Design.md §9.7 marked resolved; impl.md open-questions row updated. Strict parity for the upcoming web-review harness.
